mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost household consumption and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding residential mortgage debt indicate accelerating household balance sheet expansion and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, or the interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost inflation and supports equity valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are flat or negative when INDPRO expands, or the signal reverses direction during supply-side bottlenecks, indicating a breakdown of the capacity-expansion mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly increases corporate revenue expectations and earnings per share, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as analysts update forecasts.
failure_condition: The correlation between GDPC1 revisions and 5-day SPX returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero across multiple decades, or earnings revisions fail to follow GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.2 percent triggers market expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold, or bond yields fail to rise in response, indicating the market does not price in tightening expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts reading above 700 thousand units signals robust construction activity and household formation, which boosts related industrial demand and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative when HOUST exceeds 700, or the signal fails to predict returns after accounting for interest rate movements and broader GDP growth.
